'''Daniel Addy''' (born 15 January 1991) is a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and spent time on loan from [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the Championship. He has also played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Super League and the [[RFL Championship|second tier]], and the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Earlier in his career he played as a {{rlp|so|sh}} and {{rlp|hk}}.

==Background==
Addy was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Addy is a product of the [[Bradford Bulls]]' Academy System.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Senior career===
====Bradford Bulls (2010-16)====
=====2010=====
After his impressive début season with the [[Bradford Bulls]] in 2010, at the end of that same 2010 [[rugby league]] season, Addy signed a new two-year contract at the Bradford club.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

=====2012=====
In 2012, Addy signed a one-year extension to his current Bradford contract.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

=====2013=====
At the end of the 2013 season he signed another two-year contract extension, to stay with the Bradford club.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

=====2014=====
Addy signed a new two-year deal with the Bradford Bulls side at the end of their 2014 campaign, despite their relegation to the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

=====2015=====
In 2015, Addy played in the [[Million Pound Game]] against the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], as Bradford Bulls made a bid to return to the [[Super League]] following their relegation the season prior. Their efforts came up short, as Wakefield retained their position in the [[Super League]], by beating their [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] challengers 24-16 at [[Belle Vue (Wakefield)|Belle Vue]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

=====2016=====
The 2016 season proved to be the last year that Addy would represent Bradford, before he joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

====Dewsbury Rams (2010)====
=====2010=====
Addy appeared for the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the 2010 [[rugby league]] season, as part of Bradford's dual-registration partnership with the club.

====Hull Kingston Rovers (2017 - 2019)====
=====2017=====
Following Bradford's [[liquidation]], Addy signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/hull-kr-confirm-captures-of-bradford-bulls-duo-mitch-clark-and-danny-addy/story-29819177-detail/story.html|title=Hull KR confirm captures of Bradford Bulls duo Mitch Clark and Danny Addy|work=[[Hull Daily Mail]]|date=18 October 2016|access-date=20 February 2017}}{{Dead link|date=July 2019 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>
Addy made his Hull Kingston Rovers début on 5 February 2017, in a 54-24 victory over his former club the [[Bradford Bulls]] on the opening day of the 2017 [[rugby league]] season. Addy was one of Hull Kingston Rovers standout players in their 2017 [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] campaign, playing in numerous creative positions under Head Coach, [[Tim Sheens]].
Addy was part of the Hull Kingston Rovers side that won promotion back to the [[Super League]], at the first time of asking following relegation the season prior.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

=====2018=====
Following Hull Kingston Rovers promotion back to the [[Super League]] for the 2018 season, Addy unfortunately suffered a season-ending [[anterior cruciate ligament]] (ACL) knee injury, in a pre-season friendly against cross-city rivals [[Hull F.C.]] Head Coach [[Tim Sheens]] who later said, '''"It was one of the hardest phone calls I’ve ever had to make. Having to call Danny to say we have to go without your services this year was truly heartbreaking."'''{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

=====2019=====
On 13 January 2019, after missing the entire 2018 [[Super League]] season, Addy made his long awaited return to the Hull Kingston Rovers fold in a pre-season friendly against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], Addy claimed a 30–16 victory in his return to the side.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

====Leigh Centurions====
Addy was appointed vice-captain.

====Salford Red Devils====
On 19 December 2020 it was announced that the Leigh vice-captain had signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12167149/danny-addy-salford-red-devils-sign-scotland-international|title=Danny Addy: Salford Red Devils sign Scotland international|website=Sky Sports|access-date=19 Dec 2020|date=19 Dec 2020}}</ref>

====Featherstone Rovers====
On 3 November 2023 it was reported that he will join hometown club [[Featherstone Rovers]] for 2024.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=3 Nov 2023|title=Danny Addy joins hometown club following Salford Red Devils departure: 'This club is in my blood'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-addy-joins-hometown-club-featherstone-rovers-following-salford-red-devils-departure}}</ref>

====North Wales Crusaders====
On 17 January 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[North Wales Crusaders]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=17 Jan 2026|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Ex-Featherstone Rovers captain joins North Wales Crusaders|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-featherstone-rovers-captain-joins-north-wales-crusaders/}}</ref>

====Dewsbury Rams (rejoin)====
On 19 March 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 Mar 2026|accessdate=20 Mar 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Danny Addy leaves club after two months with next move announced|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-addy-leaves-club-after-two-months-with-next-move-announced}}</ref>

===International career (2013-17)===
====2013====
Addy was named as part of the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].

He is a current [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] International and he scored a try and kicked a goal on his début against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]], in a [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] warm-up match on 19 October 2013, which ended in a 20-38 loss to the [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Kumuls]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15319/8949738/scotland-name-six-uncapped-players-in-world-cup-squad|title=Scotland name six uncapped players in World Cup squad}}</ref>
[[File:Danny Addy 2013 RLWC.jpg|thumb|right|Addy in action for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]]]
Addy featured in the group games against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]], [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] (in which he scored a try) and the [[United States national rugby league team|USA]]. Addy also played in the Quarter-Final against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]].

====2014====
Addy was also selected in the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] squad for the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]]. He played at {{rlp|hk}}, in the tournaments opening game against [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]]. He then featured against [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] (in which he scored a try) and [[France national rugby league team|France]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

====2015====
Addy was selected in the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] squad for the [[2015 European Cup]].

He started at {{rlp|sh}}, alongside [[Danny Brough]] for the opening match against [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] and then at {{rlp|lf}} against [[France national rugby league team|France]], (in which he scored a brace of tries).{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

====2017====
Addy was selected in the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]  side for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].

He featured against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]], [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]]. His scoring record against those particular teams during the tournament was: [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] (1 try), [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] (1 goal) and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] (3 goals).{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==Coaching career==
===Scotland===
===(Scotland under-19's 2018)===
===2018===
{{BLP unreferenced section|date=February 2020}}
It was revealed on 18 April 2018, that Addy had taken over the leadership of [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]'s under-19's programme for the rest of 2018. The offer made to Addy, was to keep him busy during his long recovery from his season ending ACL knee injury. That he sustained in the pre-season of the 2018 [[rugby league]] campaign. Following the announcement of the news, Addy had this to say, "I am incredibly proud to represent and support [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]. I have been on at the Chairman, Keith Hogg, for some time to find a way for me to get involved in coaching. Obviously, I didn't want to get seriously injured but it has given me the chance to take-up this great opportunity. Furthermore he added, "I am very grateful to my club, ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) for releasing me to do this. To get the chance to develop my [[rugby league]] coaching experience within the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] set-up and help some of [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]'s younger players to develop is very exciting."{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

[[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]'s Chairman, Keith Hogg, added, "Danny is one of a large group of players whose commitment to the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Bravehearts]] is outstanding and who want to put something back into the development of the sport in Scotland. "We did not want to see him get injured, but with great support from [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] we are really pleased to have him leading our under-19's programme for the rest of 2018."{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}
